title: Brave at a New School
lesson: Trying something new feels scary at first, but being brave helps you find new friends and fun.
prompts: At a friendly school front gate in the morning — a low brick wall, black metal gate, small flower planters, soft morning light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image clutches a small backpack strap close to their chest, shoulders slightly hunched, with a nervous, uncertain expression. Beside them, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_PARENT} holds one of the child's hands gently and bends slightly toward them, looking reassuring. Make the hand-holding comfort read physically connected: both people face each other, with hands and gaze directed to the single joined-hands action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

At a friendly school front gate in the morning — a low brick wall, black metal gate, small flower planters, soft morning light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image takes one small step toward the school path, one hand still on the backpack strap and the other hand lifted in a tiny thumbs-up, with a brave, accepting smile. Beside them, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_PARENT} kneels and points warmly toward the school doors, watching with encouragement. Make the encouragement read physically connected: the parent gestures toward the doors while the child steps in that direction, both gazes following the school path. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

At a colourful classroom doorway in the morning — cubby shelves, paper stars on the door, warm classroom light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands at the doorway holding one backpack strap, body angled toward the teacher, reaching one hand toward her with a hopeful shy smile. {GENDER_F_AGE_30} kneels near the doorway at the child's level, one hand over her heart and the other hand open in a welcoming gesture, smiling warmly. Make the teacher connection read physically connected: both people face each other, with gaze and reaching hands aimed toward the greeting space between them. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm classroom light — exactly three children in total, each a single distinct child, no extra or duplicate child, gather in a small inward-facing semicircle saying hello. The {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands closest to the viewer with body turned toward the two classmates, looking directly at them and lifting one hand in a small friendly wave, with a newly brave smile. Facing the child, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} waves back with one hand and holds a picture book in the other, smiling warmly. Beside her, {GENDER_M_AGE_06} leans slightly toward the child and raises one hand in greeting, smiling back. Make the hello read as one shared interaction: the child's gaze, wave, shoulders and feet point toward the two classmates, and both classmates' gazes and waves point back toward the child, not toward the camera. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm classroom light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits at a low craft table, leaning toward one shared sheet of drawing paper and colouring one side with a crayon, with a relaxed happy smile. Beside them, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits at the craft table, colouring the other side of the single shared drawing paper and sliding a crayon toward the child, smiling like a new friend. Make the drawing together read physically connected: both children look at the one shared paper between them, with hands and crayons aimed into one drawing space. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

At a friendly school pickup gate in the afternoon — a low brick wall, black metal gate, small flower planters, golden afternoon light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image runs directly toward {GENDER_F_AGE_35_PARENT}, holding up a colourful paper drawing in one hand, with a big joyful smile. {GENDER_F_AGE_35_PARENT} kneels at the gate with open arms, smiling proudly and looking at the child's drawing. Make the pickup reunion read physically connected: the child runs into the parent's open arms while the drawing is visible between them, and both faces are turned toward each other. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}
gists: At the school front gate in morning light, the child clutches a small backpack nervously while the mother holds their hand reassuringly.
At the school front gate, the child accepts the challenge by taking a small brave step toward the school path while the mother kneels and encourages them.
At the classroom doorway, the child connects with the teacher through a shy greeting while the teacher kneels warmly to welcome them.
Inside the bright classroom, the child smiles and says hi to two classmates who wave back in a friendly greeting.
Inside the bright classroom at a craft table, the child draws on one shared paper with a new friend, both children relaxed and happy.
At afternoon pickup by the school gate, the child runs happily to Mum with a drawing, proud that bravery led to a good day.
texts: {NAME} stood at the school gate and held the backpack strap tight. "My tummy feels wobbly," whispered {NAME}. "New places can feel big," said Mum, squeezing {NAME}'s hand. "I'll stay with you for this first brave breath."
Mum knelt beside the gate and pointed to the school doors. "Are you ready to try?" asked Mum. {NAME} took one little step. "I feel nervous, but I'll try," said {NAME}.
At the classroom door, the teacher knelt with a kind smile. "Hello, {NAME}. I'm your teacher," she said. "Can I come in with you?" asked {NAME}. "Yes," said the teacher. "We'll do it together."
Inside the classroom, two children looked up from the rug. "Hi," said {NAME}, giving a small wave. "Hi, {NAME}!" called the girl. "Come sit with us," said the boy.
At the craft table, a new friend slid over a crayon. "Want to draw a rainbow with me?" she asked. "Yes please," said {NAME}. Soon their picture was full of bright colours.
When Mum came for pickup, {NAME} ran to her with the drawing held high. "I said hi and made a friend!" said {NAME}. "You were so brave," said Mum. Trying had turned scary into happy.
